title: Wall Signature: Localisation by Observation

runtime.expected_outcome: Given the 4-bit wall signature observed at each tile, entropy of the posterior drops from log(N) toward ~0 within a few steps.

symbols.wall signature: 4-bit code (N,E,S,W) used as part of the observation

audiences.kid: Every wall around you is a clue. When the clues line up, you know exactly where you are.
audiences.real: Each tile's 4-bit wall signature is usually unique in the corridor maze, so a single observation is often enough to localise.
audiences.equation: Entropy H drops toward 0 as the posterior q(s|o) concentrates. The speed of drop is a measurable diagnostic.
audiences.derivation: Conditional entropy H[s|o] = H[s] - I[s;o]. The mutual information between state and wall-signature observation determines how fast uncertainty collapses.

orchestrate.objective: Quantify how fast the posterior entropy collapses under wall-signature observations.
orchestrate.context: corridor_turns 5x5 maze; 1 observation per tile, no action.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 4.
